_about: Conformance vectors for per-playlist-item schedule evaluation. The contract: every implementation (JS server/web, Kotlin, Tizen JS) must agree with every vector. If an implementation disagrees, the implementation is wrong.

_conventions.days: active days as a set of integers 0-6, where 0=Sunday .. 6=Saturday (matches JS Date.getDay()).
_conventions.time: start/end are local wall-clock HH:MM (24h). A window is [start, end): start inclusive, end exclusive (so adjacent dayparts 07:00-11:00 and 11:00-14:00 do not overlap at 11:00). end may be "24:00" meaning end-of-day (whole day when paired with 00:00).
_conventions.overnight: if start > end the window crosses midnight: active [start,24:00) on the start day and [00:00,end) on the next day. The day-of-week AND date-range tests apply to the day the window STARTED (a Fri 22:00-02:00 block is active Sat 01:00 because it anchors to Friday).
_conventions.dates: start_date/end_date are local YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive on both ends, evaluated against the device-LOCAL calendar date. null start_date = no lower bound; null end_date = no upper bound.
_conventions.block_match: within a block, day AND date AND time must all pass. blocks OR together. >=1 matching block = active. zero blocks = always active.
_conventions.evaluation: take utc_now, convert to device-local via the IANA timezone, then test the wall-clock block(s).

vectors.description: plain daytime window, inside (Syd Fri 08:00 in Mon-Fri 07:00-11:00)
plain daytime window, outside after end (Syd Thu 12:00)
time start is INCLUSIVE (Syd Fri 07:00 exactly)
time end is EXCLUSIVE (Syd Fri 11:00 exactly -> out)
overnight wrap, before midnight on the start day (Syd Fri 22:30, Fri 22:00-02:00)
overnight wrap, after midnight anchored to start day (Syd Sat 01:30 belongs to Fri block)
overnight wrap, after midnight but anchor day (yesterday=Sat) not active (Syd Sun 01:30, block Fri)
overnight wrap, end is exclusive at 02:00 (Syd Sat 02:00 exactly -> out)
overnight wrap, before midnight on a non-active day (Syd Thu 22:30, block Fri)
date range: day before start_date (Syd local 06-11, range 06-12..06-14)
date range: on start_date inclusive (Syd local 06-12)
date range: on end_date inclusive (Syd local 06-14)
date range: day after end_date (Syd local 06-15)
null start_date = no lower bound (Syd local 2026-01-01, end 06-14)
null end_date = no upper bound (Syd local 2026-12-31, start 06-12)
both dates null = unbounded (always, given day/time ok)
date in range but wrong weekday (Syd Fri 06-12, block Mon only)
right weekday but date out of range (Syd Fri 06-19, block Fri but range 06-12..06-12)
multiple blocks OR: second block matches (Syd Sat 10:00)
multiple blocks OR: none match (Syd Sat 14:00)
zero blocks = always on
tz correctness: same UTC, Sydney -> 09:00 in window
tz correctness: same UTC, Berlin -> 01:00 out of window
tz correctness: same UTC, Chicago -> 18:00 out of window
tz correctness: different UTC, Chicago -> 11:00 in window
tz correctness: different UTC, Berlin -> 18:00 out of window
tz correctness: different UTC, Sydney -> 02:00 out of window
DST US spring-forward: after the gap, local 03:00 CDT in 03:00-04:00
DST US spring-forward: before the gap, local 01:30 CST out of 03:00-04:00
DST US spring-forward: window inside the 02:00-03:00 hole is unreachable (local 03:00)
DST US fall-back: first 01:30 (CDT) in 01:00-02:00
DST US fall-back: repeated 01:30 (CST) still in 01:00-02:00
DST US fall-back: after the repeated hour, local 02:30 out of 01:00-02:00
DST AU spring-forward: after the gap, Sydney local 03:30 AEDT in 03:00-04:00
DST AU spring-forward: before the gap, Sydney local 01:30 AEST out of 03:00-04:00
DST AU fall-back: first 02:30 (AEDT) in 02:00-03:00
DST AU fall-back: repeated 02:30 (AEST) still in 02:00-03:00
device-local date AFTER utc date (UTC 06-11 15:00 = Syd 06-12 01:00); date range tests local date
device-local date BEFORE utc date (UTC 06-12 03:00 = Chicago 06-11 22:00); date range tests local date
